title: Chest pain and stroke no-delay board
summary: Focused extreme medical support for chest pain and stroke no-delay board in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Heart attack and stroke signs need immediate transport decisions; waiting for certainty can cost the treatment window. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Chest pain and stroke no-delay board”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Chest pain and stroke no-delay board”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Chest pain and stroke no-delay board”.
paper and pen. Context: “Chest pain and stroke no-delay board”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Chest pain and stroke no-delay board»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Chest pain and stroke no-delay board»
steps: Treat chest pressure, severe shortness of breath, sweating, faintness, one-sided weakness, face droop, speech trouble, sudden severe headache, or new confusion as urgent.
Do not let the person drive themselves.
Keep them calm, seated or positioned comfortably.
Send medicine list and allergies with them.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Chest pain and stroke no-delay board”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Chest pain and stroke no-delay board”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Chest pain and stroke no-delay board”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Chest pain and stroke no-delay board”.

title: Seizure cluster escalation card
summary: Focused extreme medical support for seizure cluster escalation card in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Most seizure support is about safety, timing, recovery position, and escalation when seizures repeat or do not stop. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Seizure cluster escalation card”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Seizure cluster escalation card”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Seizure cluster escalation card”.
paper and pen. Context: “Seizure cluster escalation card”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Seizure cluster escalation card»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Seizure cluster escalation card»
steps: Protect from nearby hazards without holding the person down.
Do not put anything in the mouth.
After movement stops, keep airway clear and monitor breathing.
Escalate for first seizure, injury, pregnancy, water exposure, repeated seizures, long duration, breathing trouble, or not waking normally.
